---
title: "HB_dynatop6"
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
Difference from dynatop5:
- settle on just soils and TWI determining HRUs
- parameterize 3 different parameter sets for subsurface characteristics for 3 soil types

How well this process based model can capture the presence and absence of flow?

```{r setup}
# Load packages
library(pacman)
p_load(terra, xts, whitebox, tidyverse,
       lubridate,
       dynatopGIS,
       sf, # working with st and sf objects
       tidyterra, # do tidy ops on terra objects
       sfnetworks, # create start and end nodes for channel network
       lwgeom,
       lhs, DEoptim, # maybe obsolete since I am using GLUE
       parallel,
       readr
       )

# Load modified version of dynatop, which includes snow routine
devtools::load_all(file.path("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/HBTopModel/revised_package/dynatop")) ## attach the revised package

crs1 <- "PROJCRS[\"NAD83 / UTM zone 19N\",\n    BASEGEOGCRS[\"NAD83\",\n        DATUM[\"North American Datum 1983\",\n            ELLIPSOID[\"GRS 1980\",6378137,298.257222101,\n                LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]]],\n        PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,\n            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433]],\n        ID[\"EPSG\",4269]],\n    CONVERSION[\"UTM zone 19N\",\n        METHOD[\"Transverse Mercator\",\n            ID[\"EPSG\",9807]],\n        PARAMETER[\"Latitude of natural origin\",0,\n            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8801]],\n        PARAMETER[\"Longitude of natural origin\",-69,\n            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8802]],\n        PARAMETER[\"Scale factor at natural origin\",0.9996,\n            SCALEUNIT[\"unity\",1],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8805]],\n        PARAMETER[\"False easting\",500000,\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8806]],\n        PARAMETER[\"False northing\",0,\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8807]]],\n    CS[Cartesian,2],\n        AXIS[\"(E)\",east,\n            ORDER[1],\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]],\n        AXIS[\"(N)\",north,\n            ORDER[2],\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]],\n    USAGE[\n        SCOPE[\"Engineering survey, topographic mapping.\"],\n        AREA[\"North America - between 72°W and 66°W - onshore and offshore. Canada - Labrador; New Brunswick; Nova Scotia; Nunavut; Quebec. Puerto Rico. United States (USA) - Connecticut; Maine; Massachusetts; New Hampshire; New York (Long Island); Rhode Island; Vermont.\"],\n        BBOX[14.92,-72,84,-66]],\n    ID[\"EPSG\",26919]]"
```

# GIS PREP
Aggregate to coarser spatial resolution
```{r GIS files}
target_folder <- "./5th_attempt/unprocessed/"
dem_path <- file.path(".", "hb_data", "unprocessed",
                      "1m hydro enforced dem","hydem1mlpns.tif")
dem <- rast(dem_path) # load the dem as a raster layer

# crop DEM to a rough rectangle
ybounds <- c(4870350,4871350)
xbounds <- c(281350, 282150)
crop1 <- crop(dem, ext(c(xbounds, ybounds)))
plot(crop1)

dem3 <- terra::aggregate(crop1, 5, fun = "mean")
#save raster, because whitebox wants it is a files location instead of an object in R
crop_3mdem_path <- paste0(target_folder, "3mdem.tif")
writeRaster(dem3, crop_3mdem_path, overwrite = TRUE)

####delineate watershed and keep watershed boundary

w3_breached <- paste0(target_folder,"3mdem_breach.tif")
wbt_breach_depressions_least_cost(
  dem = crop_3mdem_path,
  output = w3_breached,
  dist = 5,
  fill = TRUE)

w3_filled <- paste0(target_folder,"3mdem_fill.tif")
wbt_fill_depressions_wang_and_liu(
  dem = w3_breached,
  output = w3_filled
)
#calculate flow accumulation and direction
w3_flowacc <- paste0(target_folder,"3mdem_flowacc.tif")
wbt_d8_flow_accumulation(input = w3_filled,
                         output = w3_flowacc)
w3_d8pt <- paste0(target_folder,"3mdem_d8pt.tif")
wbt_d8_pointer(dem = w3_filled,
               output = w3_d8pt)

#delineate streams
w3_streams <- paste0(target_folder,"streams.tif")
wbt_extract_streams(flow_accum = w3_flowacc,
                    output = w3_streams,
                    threshold = 200)
plot(rast(w3_streams))

# convert streams to shapefile
w3_streams_vect <- paste0(target_folder,"streams_vect.shp")
wbt_raster_streams_to_vector(streams = w3_streams,
                             d8_pntr = w3_d8pt,
                             output = w3_streams_vect)
plot(vect(w3_streams_vect))

# get pour point ready
pour_point_path <- file.path(".", "hb_data",
                             "unprocessed",
                             "1m hydro enforced dem", "w3_pour_snap.shp")
plot(vect(pour_point_path), add = TRUE)

w3_pour_snap <- paste0(target_folder,"pour.shp")
wbt_jenson_snap_pour_points(pour_pts = pour_point_path,
                            streams = w3_streams,
                            output = w3_pour_snap,
                            snap_dist = 10)
w3_pour_snap_read <- vect(w3_pour_snap)
plot(rast(w3_streams), 
     xlim = c(281400, 282000),
     ylim = c(4870400, 4870800))
points(w3_pour_snap_read, pch = 1)


w3_shed <- paste0(target_folder,"shed.tif")
wbt_watershed(d8_pntr = w3_d8pt,
              pour_pts = w3_pour_snap,
              output = w3_shed)

plot(rast(w3_shed))
#convert raster of watershed area to vector for final mapping
w3_outline <- as.polygons(rast(w3_shed), extent=FALSE)
plot(w3_outline)

plot(w3_outline)

channel <- vect(w3_streams_vect, crs = crs1) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline)


# crop and mask DEM by watershed outline
dem_crop_mask <- rast(w3_filled) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline) %>% 
  mask(w3_outline)

#create watershed area, just use area of DEM
outline <- (dem_crop_mask * 0) + 1
# extend both rasters by 1 pixel
outline_extended <- extend(outline,1)
dem_final <- extend(dem_crop_mask,1)

# STIC locations
all_24 <- read_csv("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/VT Research/HBTopModel/STIC_Synthesis/all_24.csv")

stic_locs <- all_24 %>%
  filter(wshed == "W3") %>%
  select(number, lat, long) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  vect(geom = c("long", "lat"), crs = "EPSG:4326") %>%
  project("EPSG:26919")
# save STIC locations, un-snapped
stic_locs_not_snapped <- paste0(target_folder,"not_snapped_stics.shp")
writeVector(stic_locs, stic_locs_not_snapped, overwrite = TRUE)
# snap STIC sensors to channel network
stic_locs_snapped <- paste0(target_folder,"snapped_stics.shp")

wbt_jenson_snap_pour_points(pour_pts = stic_locs_not_snapped,
                            streams = w3_streams,
                            output = stic_locs_snapped,
                            snap_dist = 30)
ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatvector(data = w3_outline) +
    geom_spatvector(data = channel, color = "blue") +
  geom_spatvector(data = vect(stic_locs_snapped), color = "black")+
  theme_void()

processed_folder <- "./5th_attempt/processed/"

writeRaster(outline_extended, paste0(processed_folder, "w3.tif"),overwrite=TRUE)
writeRaster(dem_final,paste0(processed_folder, "dem.tif"),overwrite=TRUE)
```
```{r build channel}
# =============================================================================
# build_dynatop_channel.R  (v3 — definitive)
#
# ROOT CAUSE (discovered from HRU 39 endNode=30, sf_flow_direction=47):
#   convert_channel sets each channel HRU's id    = startNode value
#                                    sf_flow_dir  = endNode value
#   It never uses the 'name' column for ordering.
#
# Therefore the startNode/endNode values in the SpatVector passed to
# convert_channel MUST themselves be topologically ordered integers:
#   outlet segment startNode = 1 (lowest)
#   every upstream segment startNode > its downstream segment's startNode
#
# All previous scripts sorted rows and assigned 'name' = 1..N but left the
# original sfnetwork-derived node ids in startNode/endNode untouched.
# Those arbitrary node ids (e.g. 30, 47) are what convert_channel actually
# uses, so the ordering we imposed on 'name' had no effect.
#
# This script replaces startNode/endNode with fresh topological ids BEFORE
# calling convert_channel.
# =============================================================================

locs <- vect(stic_locs_snapped)

## They've been trying to stop the wave, but the wave don't stop
channel <- vect(w3_streams_vect, crs = crs1) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline) %>% 
    mutate(ID = seq(1, n(), 1))

plot(channel)

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = channel)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = channel, aes(label = ID))+
  geom_spatvector(data = locs, color = "blue")
channel_trimmed <- channel %>%
  filter(!ID %in% c(1, 8,9, 12, 19, 4, 39, 25, 15))

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = channel_trimmed)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = channel_trimmed, aes(label = ID))+
    geom_spatvector(data = locs, color = "blue")

sv <- channel_trimmed %>% 
  select(ID)
sv_sf <- st_as_sf(sv)

# Function to merge selected names into single row
names_to_merge1 <- c(3,2,7,6)

# inputs:
#' @param names_to_merge Identifying column of lines to merge
#' @param sv_sf SV converted to SF to combine elements of
#' @return all_rows, combined sf object
# Don't renumber segments, so several can be chained together from initial assessment
merge_segments <- function(names_to_merge, sv_sf){
  others <- sv_sf[!sv_sf$ID %in% names_to_merge, ]
  to_merge <- sv_sf[sv_sf$ID %in% names_to_merge, ]
  
  merged_geom <- st_union(st_geometry(to_merge))
  
  merged_row <- st_sf(
    ID = names_to_merge[1],
    geometry = merged_geom)
  
  all_rows <- rbind(merged_row, others)
  return(all_rows)
}

test <- merge_segments(names_to_merge = c(3,2,7,6),
                       sv_sf = sv_sf)
vect_final <- vect(test) %>% 
  # renumber segments
  mutate(ID = row_number())

ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatvector(data = vect_final) +
  geom_spatvector_text(data = vect_final, aes(label = ID))+
  geom_spatvector(data = stic_locs, color = "blue")#+
  lims(x = c(281500, 281700),
       y = c(4870400, 4870500))+
  theme_minimal() 

# writeVector(
#   vect_final,
#   file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "channel.shp"),
#   overwrite = TRUE
# )

test2 <- terra::aggregate(vect_final)

new_data <- data.frame(ID = 1)
values(test2) <- new_data
# =============================================================================
# Main function
# =============================================================================

#' Build a topologically correct channel SpatVector for dynatop
#'
#' @param channel_sv       SpatVector of directed channel lines (upstream to downstream).
#' @param split_points_sv  SpatVector of points marking desired HRU boundaries.
#' @param snap_tolerance   Max snap distance in map units. Points beyond this warn.
#' @param node_tolerance   Coordinate precision for merging coincident endpoints.
#' @param default_width    Channel width assigned to all segments.
#' @param default_slope    Slope assigned to all segments.
#'
#' @return List with three elements:
#'   $channel_sv    SpatVector ready for convert_channel. startNode values are
#'                  topological ids (outlet = 1). endNode = downstream id (0 at outlet).
#'   $channel_df    data.frame of the same, for inspection and validation.
#'   $conv_channel  Output of convert_channel — pass directly to dynatop.

build_dynatop_channel <- function(
    channel_sv,
    split_points_sv,
    snap_tolerance = 1,
    node_tolerance = 0.01,
    default_width  = 1,
    default_slope  = 0.001
) {

  # ── 0. CRS alignment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  channel_sf <- st_cast(st_as_sf(channel_sv), "LINESTRING")
  points_sf  <- st_as_sf(split_points_sv)
  if (st_crs(channel_sf) != st_crs(points_sf)) {
    message("CRS mismatch — reprojecting split points.")
    points_sf <- st_transform(points_sf, st_crs(channel_sf))
  }
  message(sprintf("Input: %d line feature(s), %d split point(s).",
                  nrow(channel_sf), nrow(points_sf)))

  # ── 1. Snap split points to nearest position on the channel ──────────────────
  channel_union <- st_union(channel_sf)
  nearest_segs  <- st_nearest_points(points_sf, channel_union)
  snapped_pts   <- st_cast(nearest_segs, "POINT")[seq(2, 2*nrow(points_sf), 2)]

  snap_dists <- as.numeric(st_distance(
    points_sf, st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts), by_element = TRUE
  ))
  far <- which(snap_dists > snap_tolerance)
  if (length(far) > 0)
    warning(sprintf("%d point(s) >%g map units from channel (rows: %s).",
                    length(far), snap_tolerance, paste(far, collapse=",")),
            call.=FALSE)

  # ── 2. Split each line at points that fall on it ──────────────────────────────
  segment_list <- vector("list", nrow(channel_sf))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(channel_sf))) {
    this_line    <- channel_sf[i, ]
    dist_to_line <- as.numeric(st_distance(st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts), this_line))
    on_this      <- which(dist_to_line <= node_tolerance * 10)

    if (length(on_this) == 0) {
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(this_line))
    } else {
      pts_u <- st_union(snapped_pts[on_this])
      parts <- tryCatch(
        st_collection_extract(lwgeom::st_split(this_line, pts_u), "LINESTRING"),
        error = function(e) {
          warning(sprintf("Split failed on line %d — kept unsplit.", i), call.=FALSE)
          this_line
        }
      )
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(parts))
    }
  }

  all_segs <- do.call(rbind, segment_list)
  rownames(all_segs) <- NULL
  n_segs <- nrow(all_segs)
  message(sprintf("After splitting: %d segment(s).", n_segs))

  # ── 3. Build coordinate-based node topology ────────────────────────────────
  # Directed lines: first coordinate = upstream end, last = downstream end.
  precision <- max(0, ceiling(-log10(node_tolerance)) + 1)
  get_endpoints <- function(geom) {
    co <- st_coordinates(geom)
    rbind(round(co[1,        c("X","Y")], precision),
          round(co[nrow(co), c("X","Y")], precision))
  }

  ep_matrix   <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(n_segs), function(i) get_endpoints(all_segs[i,])))
  coord_keys  <- paste(ep_matrix[,"X"], ep_matrix[,"Y"])
  unique_keys <- unique(coord_keys)
  node_ids    <- match(coord_keys, unique_keys)
  start_ids   <- node_ids[seq(1, 2*n_segs, 2)]  # coord-based, arbitrary integers
  end_ids     <- node_ids[seq(2, 2*n_segs, 2)]

  # ── 4. Topological sort — BFS from outlet ────────────────────────────────────
  sn_to_row <- setNames(seq_len(n_segs), as.character(start_ids))
  ds        <- unname(sn_to_row[as.character(end_ids)])  # NA = outlet

  outlets <- which(is.na(ds))
  if (length(outlets) == 0)
    stop(paste0(
      "No outlet found — every segment's end connects to another segment's start.\n",
      "Verify that lines are directed upstream → downstream."
    ))
  if (length(outlets) > 1)
    warning(sprintf(
      "%d outlets detected — network may be disconnected or have unjoined tributaries.",
      length(outlets)
    ), call.=FALSE)

  upstream_of <- vector("list", n_segs)
  for (i in seq_len(n_segs))
    if (!is.na(ds[i])) upstream_of[[ds[i]]] <- c(upstream_of[[ds[i]]], i)

  visited   <- logical(n_segs)
  new_order <- integer(0)
  queue     <- outlets
  while (length(queue) > 0) {
    cur <- queue[1]; queue <- queue[-1]
    if (!visited[cur]) {
      visited[cur] <- TRUE
      new_order    <- c(new_order, cur)
      queue        <- c(queue, upstream_of[[cur]])
    }
  }

  unreachable <- setdiff(seq_len(n_segs), new_order)
  if (length(unreachable) > 0)
    warning(sprintf(
      "%d segment(s) unreachable from the outlet — possible cycle or disconnected sub-network.",
      length(unreachable)
    ), call.=FALSE)

  n_sorted <- length(new_order)

  # ── 5. THE KEY STEP: replace startNode/endNode with topological ids ───────────
  #
  # convert_channel uses startNode as each HRU's id, and endNode as its
  # sf_flow_direction. So these values — not 'name', not row order — must be
  # topologically ordered so that endNode < startNode for every non-outlet.
  #
  # BFS order gives us: new_order[1] = outlet row, new_order[n] = headwaters.
  # Assign topo_id = position in new_order, so outlet gets topo_id = 1.
  # Each segment's downstream row has a LOWER topo_id by construction.

  topo_id   <- seq_len(n_sorted)  # 1 = outlet, n_sorted = farthest headwater

  # Find downstream topo_id for each sorted segment
  sorted_start_orig <- start_ids[new_order]
  sorted_end_orig   <- end_ids[new_order]
  sn_orig_to_sorted_row <- setNames(seq_len(n_sorted), as.character(sorted_start_orig))
  ds_sorted <- unname(sn_orig_to_sorted_row[as.character(sorted_end_orig)])  # NA = outlet

  # New startNode = this segment's topo_id
  # New endNode   = downstream segment's topo_id; 0 for outlet
  new_startNode <- topo_id
  new_endNode   <- ifelse(is.na(ds_sorted), 0L, topo_id[ds_sorted])

  # Guarantee: for all non-outlet, new_endNode < new_startNode
  # Because ds_sorted[i] < i always (BFS visits downstream before upstream)
  violations <- which(new_endNode != 0 & new_endNode >= new_startNode)
  if (length(violations) > 0)
    stop(sprintf(
      "BUG: topological id assignment still has violations at sorted rows: %s.\n",
      paste(violations, collapse=", ")
    ), "Please file a bug report.")

  sorted_lengths <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs[new_order, ]))

  # ── 6. Assemble output ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  channel_df <- data.frame(
    name      = topo_id,          # label = id for clarity; convert_channel reads this as label only
    startNode = new_startNode,    # THIS is what becomes the HRU id in dynatop
    endNode   = new_endNode,      # THIS is what becomes sf_flow_direction
    length    = sorted_lengths,
    width     = default_width,
    slope     = default_slope,
    area      = sorted_lengths * default_width,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  sorted_geom    <- st_geometry(all_segs[new_order, ])
  channel_sf_out <- st_sf(channel_df, geometry = sorted_geom)
  channel_sv_out <- vect(channel_sf_out)
  crs(channel_sv_out) <- crs(channel_sv)

  # ── 7. Pre-flight validation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  validate_channel_sv(channel_sv_out, verbose = TRUE)

  # ── 8. Run convert_channel ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  conv_channel <- convert_channel(
    channel_sv_out,
    property_names = c(
      name      = "name",
      length    = "length",
      startNode = "startNode",
      endNode   = "endNode"
    ),
    default_width = default_width,
    default_slope = default_slope
  )

  list(
    channel_sv   = channel_sv_out,
    channel_df   = channel_df,
    conv_channel = conv_channel
  )
}


# =============================================================================
# Validation: checks the rule as convert_channel sees it
#   id = startNode; sf_flow_direction = endNode
#   => endNode must be strictly less than startNode for every non-outlet row
# =============================================================================

#' Validate a channel SpatVector before passing to convert_channel
#'
#' @param sv       SpatVector with startNode, endNode columns.
#' @param verbose  Print results.
#' @return Logical TRUE (pass) / FALSE (fail), invisibly.

validate_channel_sv <- function(sv, verbose = TRUE) {
  df <- as.data.frame(sv)

  outlet_rows <- df$endNode == 0 | !df$endNode %in% df$startNode
  bad         <- which(!outlet_rows & df$endNode >= df$startNode)

  if (verbose) {
    message(sprintf(
      "Segments: %d | Outlet startNode(s): %s | Violations: %d",
      nrow(df),
      paste(df$startNode[outlet_rows], collapse=", "),
      length(bad)
    ))
    if (length(bad) > 0) {
      message("Violations (endNode must be < startNode for non-outlet segments):")
      print(df[bad, c("name","startNode","endNode")], row.names=FALSE)
    } else {
      message("PASS — startNode/endNode ordering is correct for convert_channel and dynatop.")
    }
  }
  invisible(length(bad) == 0)
}


# =============================================================================
# Usage
# =============================================================================
#
result <- build_dynatop_channel(
  channel_sv      = test2,
  split_points_sv = locs,
  snap_tolerance  = 20,
  node_tolerance  = 0.01,
  default_width   = 1,
  default_slope   = 0.001
)

# writeVector(
#   result$conv_channel,
#   file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "channel.shp"),
#   overwrite = TRUE
# )

# # Inspect the final table — startNode IS the HRU id, endNode IS sf_flow_direction
print(result$channel_df)
plot(result$channel_sv)

plot(st_as_sf(result$channel_sv))
#
# # result$conv_channel is already the convert_channel output — use directly:
# ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(hru)   # where hru includes result$conv_channel
#
# # You can also re-validate at any time:
 validate_channel_sv(result$channel_sv)
 
 ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatvector(data = result$channel_sv) +
  geom_spatvector_text(data = result$channel_sv, aes(label = name))
  
  # geom_text(data = centroid_coords[4,], aes(x = x, y = y, label = hru_id), 
  #           color = "black", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  # Use a nice color palette and remove the bulky legend
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", na.value = "transparent", guide = "none") +
  lims(x = c(281500, 281700),
       y = c(4870400, 4870500))+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Hubbard Brook HRU & Channel ID Map",
       subtitle = "Hillslopes (1-20) and Channels (21+)",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude")
```

```{r claude channel fixer}

# Function to merge selected names into single row
merge_segments <- function(names_to_merge, sv_sf, snap_tolerance = 0.1){
  others   <- sv_sf[!sv_sf$ID %in% names_to_merge, ]
  to_merge <- sv_sf[ sv_sf$ID %in% names_to_merge, ]
  
  # Snap before union to close sub-mm endpoint gaps
  merged_geom <- st_snap(st_geometry(to_merge),
                          st_geometry(to_merge),
                          tolerance = snap_tolerance)
  
  # st_line_merge turns connected MULTILINESTRING into single LINESTRING
  merged_geom <- st_line_merge(st_union(merged_geom))
  
  if(st_geometry_type(merged_geom) == "MULTILINESTRING")
    warning(paste("Segments", paste(names_to_merge, collapse=","),
                  "did not form a single connected line — check they share endpoints",
                  "or increase snap_tolerance."))
  
  merged_row <- st_sf(ID = names_to_merge[1], geometry = merged_geom)
  rbind(merged_row, others)
}

# =============================================================================
# Helper: split a line at a fractional position along its length
# Returns a list of two sf lines (upstream part, downstream part)
# =============================================================================
split_line_at_fraction <- function(line_sf, frac){
  # frac in [0,1]: 0 = start, 1 = end of line
  # Uses lwgeom::st_linesubstring
  if(frac <= 0 || frac >= 1) return(list(line_sf, line_sf))
  
  part1 <- lwgeom::st_linesubstring(line_sf, 0,    frac)
  part2 <- lwgeom::st_linesubstring(line_sf, frac, 1)
  list(part1, part2)
}

# =============================================================================
# Add this helper inside build_dynatop_channel, after the splitting block
# and before "── 3. Build coordinate-based node topology ──"
# =============================================================================

merge_short_segments <- function(all_segs, min_length_m, node_tolerance){
  
  # Iteratively merge short segments into their nearest neighbour
  # until no segments below min_length_m remain
  max_iter <- 50L
  
  for(iter in seq_len(max_iter)){
    
    seg_lengths <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs))
    short_idx   <- which(seg_lengths < min_length_m)
    
    if(length(short_idx) == 0){
      message(sprintf("  merge_short_segments: done after %d pass(es).", iter - 1))
      break
    }
    
    message(sprintf("  Pass %d: merging %d segment(s) < %g m.",
                    iter, length(short_idx), min_length_m))
    
    # Process shortest segment first
    target <- short_idx[which.min(seg_lengths[short_idx])]
    
    # Find topologically connected neighbours by shared endpoint
    precision  <- max(0, ceiling(-log10(node_tolerance)) + 1)
    
    get_endpoints <- function(geom){
      co <- st_coordinates(geom)
      list(
        start = round(co[1,        c("X","Y")], precision),
        end   = round(co[nrow(co), c("X","Y")], precision)
      )
    }
    
    ep_target <- get_endpoints(all_segs[target, ])
    
    # Find segments that share an endpoint with target (excluding itself)
    neighbours <- c()
    for(i in seq_len(nrow(all_segs))){
      if(i == target) next
      ep_i <- get_endpoints(all_segs[i, ])
      # Check all four endpoint combinations
      if(
        all(abs(ep_i$start - ep_target$start) < node_tolerance) ||
        all(abs(ep_i$start - ep_target$end)   < node_tolerance) ||
        all(abs(ep_i$end   - ep_target$start) < node_tolerance) ||
        all(abs(ep_i$end   - ep_target$end)   < node_tolerance)
      ){
        neighbours <- c(neighbours, i)
      }
    }
    
    if(length(neighbours) == 0){
      # Isolated segment — remove it (no valid neighbour to merge into)
      message(sprintf("  Removing isolated segment %d (no connected neighbour).", target))
      all_segs <- all_segs[-target, ]
      next
    }
    
    # Prefer merging into the shortest neighbour to keep segments balanced
    # (avoids creating one giant segment by always merging into the longest)
    best_neighbour <- neighbours[which.min(seg_lengths[neighbours])]
    
    # Merge geometries using st_line_merge(st_union(...)) for clean LINESTRING
    merged_geom <- st_line_merge(
      st_union(
        st_snap(
          c(st_geometry(all_segs[target, ]),
            st_geometry(all_segs[best_neighbour, ])),
          c(st_geometry(all_segs[target, ]),
            st_geometry(all_segs[best_neighbour, ])),
          tolerance = node_tolerance * 10
        )
      )
    )
    
    if(st_geometry_type(merged_geom) == "MULTILINESTRING"){
      # Snap tolerance wasn't enough — try larger snap and union
      merged_geom <- st_line_merge(
        st_union(st_geometry(all_segs[target, ]),
                 st_geometry(all_segs[best_neighbour, ]))
      )
    }
    
    # Replace neighbour row with merged geometry, remove target row
    all_segs[best_neighbour, ] <- st_sf(geometry = merged_geom)
    all_segs <- all_segs[-target, ]
    rownames(all_segs) <- NULL
  }
  
  if(iter == max_iter)
    warning("merge_short_segments hit max iterations — check for disconnected geometry.")
  
  all_segs
}

# =============================================================================
# FIXED build_dynatop_channel
# Key change: split points create a ±buffer_m segment unless bounded by
# an adjacent split or line endpoint
# =============================================================================

build_dynatop_channel <- function(
    channel_sv,
    split_points_sv,
    buffer_m       = 5,        # half-length of observation segments (m)
    min_length_m   = buffer_m * 0.8, # slivers below this are merged
    snap_tolerance = 1,        # max snap distance for points to line (m)
    node_tolerance = 0.01,     # coordinate precision for endpoint matching
    default_width  = 1,
    default_slope  = 0.001
){
  
  channel_sf <- st_cast(st_as_sf(channel_sv), "LINESTRING")
  points_sf  <- st_as_sf(split_points_sv)
  
  if(st_crs(channel_sf) != st_crs(points_sf)){
    message("CRS mismatch — reprojecting split points.")
    points_sf <- st_transform(points_sf, st_crs(channel_sf))
  }
  
  message(sprintf("Input: %d line feature(s), %d split point(s).",
                  nrow(channel_sf), nrow(points_sf)))
  
  # ── 1. Snap split points to nearest position on the channel ──────────────
  channel_union <- st_union(channel_sf)
  nearest_segs  <- st_nearest_points(points_sf, channel_union)
  snapped_pts   <- st_cast(nearest_segs, "POINT")[seq(2, 2*nrow(points_sf), 2)]
  
  snap_dists <- as.numeric(st_distance(
    points_sf,
    st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts),
    by_element = TRUE
  ))
  far <- which(snap_dists > snap_tolerance)
  if(length(far) > 0)
    warning(sprintf("%d point(s) >%g m from channel (rows: %s).",
                    length(far), snap_tolerance, paste(far, collapse=",")),
            call.=FALSE)
  
  # ── 2. For each line, find which points fall on it and where ─────────────
  # For each snapped point on a line, compute fractions for:
  #   frac_pt  — position of the point itself
  #   frac_lo  — frac_pt - buffer_m/line_length  (or 0 if too close to start)
  #   frac_hi  — frac_pt + buffer_m/line_length  (or 1 if too close to end)
  # All fractions for a line are sorted and de-duplicated before splitting.
  
  segment_list <- vector("list", nrow(channel_sf))
  
  for(i in seq_len(nrow(channel_sf))){
    this_line    <- channel_sf[i, ]
    line_length  <- as.numeric(st_length(this_line))
    
    dist_to_line <- as.numeric(st_distance(
      st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts), this_line
    ))
    on_this <- which(dist_to_line <= node_tolerance * 10)
    
    if(length(on_this) == 0){
      # No split points on this line — keep as-is
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(this_line))
      next
    }
    
    # Compute fractional positions of each point along this line
    fracs_all <- c(0, 1)  # always include endpoints
    
    for(j in on_this){
      pt <- snapped_pts[j]
      
      # Project point onto line to get distance from start
      # Use st_nearest_points to find the foot of perpendicular
      nearest <- st_nearest_points(pt, st_geometry(this_line))
      foot    <- st_cast(nearest, "POINT")[2]
      
      # Distance from line start to foot
      # Compute by splitting line at foot and measuring first part
      # Use parameterized position via lwgeom
      frac_pt <- tryCatch({
        # Line substring from 0 to foot: measure its length / total length
        d_from_start <- as.numeric(
          st_length(lwgeom::st_linesubstring(this_line, 0,
            # Binary search for fraction — lwgeom doesn't give inverse directly
            # Use st_line_sample approximation then refine
            {
              # Approximate: sample many points, find closest
              n_samp  <- max(100, ceiling(line_length))
              fracs_s <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n_samp)
              samples <- st_line_sample(this_line, sample = fracs_s)
              samples_pts <- st_cast(samples, "POINT")
              dists   <- as.numeric(st_distance(foot, samples_pts))
              fracs_s[which.min(dists)]
            }
          ))
        )
        d_from_start / line_length
      }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
      
      if(is.na(frac_pt) || frac_pt < 0 || frac_pt > 1) next
      
      # ±buffer_m in fractional units, clamped to [0,1]
      frac_lo <- max(0, frac_pt - buffer_m / line_length)
      frac_hi <- min(1, frac_pt + buffer_m / line_length)
      
      # Only add interior fractions (not already at 0 or 1)
      if(frac_lo > node_tolerance)             fracs_all <- c(fracs_all, frac_lo)
      if(frac_hi < 1 - node_tolerance)         fracs_all <- c(fracs_all, frac_hi)
    }
    
    # Sort, deduplicate close fractions, split
    fracs_all <- sort(unique(fracs_all))
    fracs_all <- fracs_all[c(TRUE, diff(fracs_all) > node_tolerance / line_length)]
    
    # Build sub-segments from consecutive fraction pairs
    parts <- vector("list", length(fracs_all) - 1)
    for(k in seq_len(length(fracs_all) - 1)){
      f1 <- fracs_all[k]
      f2 <- fracs_all[k + 1]
      parts[[k]] <- tryCatch(
        lwgeom::st_linesubstring(this_line, f1, f2),
        error = function(e){
          warning(sprintf("st_linesubstring failed on line %d [%.4f, %.4f]", i, f1, f2))
          NULL
        }
      )
    }
    
    parts <- Filter(Negate(is.null), parts)
    if(length(parts) == 0){
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(this_line))
    } else {
      geoms <- do.call(c, lapply(parts, st_geometry))
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = geoms)
    }
  }
  
  all_segs <- do.call(rbind, segment_list)
  rownames(all_segs) <- NULL
  
  # Remove degenerate zero-length segments
  seg_lengths <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs))
  all_segs    <- all_segs[seg_lengths > node_tolerance, ]
  n_segs      <- nrow(all_segs)
  
  message(sprintf("After splitting at ±%gm buffers: %d segment(s).", buffer_m, n_segs))
  
  
  
  # ── 2b. Remove short remnant segments ────────────────────────────────────
  # min_length_m: segments shorter than this are merged into a neighbour.
  # Set to slightly less than buffer_m so that genuine sensor segments
  # (≈ 2*buffer_m = 10m) are never merged away, but slivers are.
  min_length_m <- buffer_m * 0.8   # e.g. 4m for buffer_m=5

  n_before <- nrow(all_segs)
  all_segs <- merge_short_segments(all_segs, min_length_m, node_tolerance)
  n_after  <- nrow(all_segs)
  
  message(sprintf("Short segment removal: %d → %d segment(s) (removed %d).",
                  n_before, n_after, n_before - n_after))
  
  
  # ── 3. Build coordinate-based node topology ───────────────────────────────
  precision <- max(0, ceiling(-log10(node_tolerance)) + 1)
  
  get_endpoints <- function(geom){
    co <- st_coordinates(geom)
    rbind(
      round(co[1,        c("X","Y")], precision),
      round(co[nrow(co), c("X","Y")], precision)
    )
  }
  
  ep_matrix   <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(n_segs),
                                        function(i) get_endpoints(all_segs[i,])))
  coord_keys  <- paste(ep_matrix[,"X"], ep_matrix[,"Y"])
  unique_keys <- unique(coord_keys)
  node_ids    <- match(coord_keys, unique_keys)
  start_ids   <- node_ids[seq(1, 2*n_segs, 2)]
  end_ids     <- node_ids[seq(2, 2*n_segs, 2)]
  
  # ── 4. Topological sort — BFS from outlet ─────────────────────────────────
  sn_to_row <- setNames(seq_len(n_segs), as.character(start_ids))
  ds        <- unname(sn_to_row[as.character(end_ids)])  # NA = outlet
  
  outlets <- which(is.na(ds))
  if(length(outlets) == 0)
    stop("No outlet found. Check that lines are directed upstream→downstream.")
  if(length(outlets) > 1)
    warning(sprintf("%d outlets detected — network may be disconnected.",
                    length(outlets)), call.=FALSE)
  
  upstream_of <- vector("list", n_segs)
  for(i in seq_len(n_segs))
    if(!is.na(ds[i])) upstream_of[[ds[i]]] <- c(upstream_of[[ds[i]]], i)
  
  visited   <- logical(n_segs)
  new_order <- integer(0)
  queue     <- outlets
  while(length(queue) > 0){
    cur <- queue[1]; queue <- queue[-1]
    if(!visited[cur]){
      visited[cur] <- TRUE
      new_order    <- c(new_order, cur)
      queue        <- c(queue, upstream_of[[cur]])
    }
  }
  
  unreachable <- setdiff(seq_len(n_segs), new_order)
  if(length(unreachable) > 0)
    warning(sprintf("%d segment(s) unreachable from outlet — check network connectivity.",
                    length(unreachable)), call.=FALSE)
  
  n_sorted <- length(new_order)
  
  # ── 5. Assign topological ids ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  topo_id              <- seq_len(n_sorted)
  sorted_start_orig    <- start_ids[new_order]
  sorted_end_orig      <- end_ids[new_order]
  sn_orig_to_sorted    <- setNames(seq_len(n_sorted),
                                    as.character(sorted_start_orig))
  ds_sorted            <- unname(sn_orig_to_sorted[as.character(sorted_end_orig)])
  
  new_startNode <- topo_id
  new_endNode   <- ifelse(is.na(ds_sorted), 0L, topo_id[ds_sorted])
  
  violations <- which(new_endNode != 0 & new_endNode >= new_startNode)
  if(length(violations) > 0)
    stop(sprintf("Topological id violations at sorted rows: %s.",
                 paste(violations, collapse=", ")))
  
  sorted_lengths <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs[new_order, ]))
  
  # ── 6. Assemble output ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  channel_df <- data.frame(
    name      = topo_id,
    startNode = new_startNode,
    endNode   = new_endNode,
    length    = sorted_lengths,
    width     = default_width,
    slope     = default_slope,
    area      = sorted_lengths * default_width,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  sorted_geom    <- st_geometry(all_segs[new_order, ])
  channel_sf_out <- st_sf(channel_df, geometry = sorted_geom)
  channel_sv_out <- vect(channel_sf_out)
  crs(channel_sv_out) <- crs(channel_sv)
  
  validate_channel_sv(channel_sv_out, verbose = TRUE)
  
  # ── 7. Run convert_channel ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  conv_channel <- convert_channel(
    channel_sv_out,
    property_names = c(
      name      = "name",
      length    = "length",
      startNode = "startNode",
      endNode   = "endNode"
    ),
    default_width = default_width,
    default_slope = default_slope
  )
  
  list(
    channel_sv   = channel_sv_out,
    channel_df   = channel_df,
    conv_channel = conv_channel
  )
}

# =============================================================================
# Validation helper (unchanged)
# =============================================================================

validate_channel_sv <- function(sv, verbose = TRUE){
  df          <- as.data.frame(sv)
  outlet_rows <- df$endNode == 0 | !df$endNode %in% df$startNode
  bad         <- which(!outlet_rows & df$endNode >= df$startNode)
  if(verbose){
    message(sprintf(
      "Segments: %d | Outlet startNode(s): %s | Violations: %d",
      nrow(df),
      paste(df$startNode[outlet_rows], collapse=", "),
      length(bad)
    ))
    if(length(bad) > 0){
      message("Violations:")
      print(df[bad, c("name","startNode","endNode")], row.names=FALSE)
    } else {
      message("PASS — ordering correct for convert_channel and dynatop.")
    }
  }
  invisible(length(bad) == 0)
}

# =============================================================================
# USAGE — adapt paths and IDs to your watershed
# =============================================================================

# ── Step 1: Remove non-existent channels ─────────────────────────────────────

locs <- vect(stic_locs_snapped)

## They've been trying to stop the wave, but the wave don't stop
channel <- vect(w3_streams_vect, crs = crs1) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline) %>% 
    mutate(ID = seq(1, n(), 1))


ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = channel)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = channel, aes(label = ID))+
  geom_spatvector(data = locs, color = "blue")

channel_trimmed <- channel %>%
  filter(!ID %in% c(1,2,8,9,4,14,30,35,45,24,27,16,12))
# 10 m resolution trimmed: c(1,5,6,4,12,7,23,35,32,41)
# 3 m resolution trimmed: c(1, 8,9, 12, 19, 4, 39, 25, 15, 2)

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = channel_trimmed)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = channel_trimmed, aes(label = ID))+
    geom_spatvector(data = locs, color = "blue")
# ── Step 2: Convert to sf ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sv_sf <- st_as_sf(channel_trimmed %>% select(ID))

# ── Step 3: Merge segments that should be one reach ──────────────────────────
# IMPORTANT: merge BEFORE renumbering — IDs must exist in sv_sf
# Use increased snap_tolerance if segments don't share exact endpoints

test <- merge_segments(names_to_merge = c(3,7,6),
                       sv_sf = sv_sf,
                       snap_tolerance = 0.1)
ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = test)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = test, aes(label = ID))+
    geom_spatvector(data = locs, color = "blue")

vect_final <- vect(test) %>% 
  # renumber segments
  mutate(ID = row_number())

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = vect_final)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = vect_final, aes(label = ID))+
    geom_spatvector(data = locs, color = "blue")

# ── Step 6: Build channel with observation point splits ──────────────────────
# split_points_sv  — your STIC/observation locations (already projected)
# buffer_m = 5     — creates a 10m segment (5m each side) at each location
#                    unless bounded by an adjacent split or line endpoint
result <- build_dynatop_channel(
  channel_sv      = vect_final,
  split_points_sv = locs,
  buffer_m        = 5,          # ← 5m either side of observation point
  min_length_m   = buffer_m * 2,
  snap_tolerance  = 20,         # max distance to snap obs point to channel
  node_tolerance  = 0.01,
  default_width   = 1,
  default_slope   = 0.001
)

# ── Step 7: Inspect ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print(result$channel_df)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = st_as_sf(result$channel_sv)) +
    geom_sf(data = locs, color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_as_sf(result$channel_sv), aes(label = name), size = 3) +
  ggtitle("final channel HRUs")+
  theme_void()
ggsave("finalHRUS.png", scale = 3)
validate_channel_sv(result$channel_sv)


```

```{r updated channel creation}
build_dynatop_channel <- function(
    channel_sv,
    split_points_sv,
    sensor_seg_m   = 10,     # total length of sensor segments (5m each side)
    fill_seg_m     = 20,     # target length of fill segments between sensors
    snap_tolerance = 20,     # max distance to snap sensor to network (m)
    node_tolerance = 0.01,   # endpoint matching precision
    default_width  = 1,
    default_slope  = 0.001
){
  channel_sf <- st_cast(st_as_sf(channel_sv), "LINESTRING")
  points_sf  <- st_as_sf(split_points_sv)
  if(st_crs(channel_sf) != st_crs(points_sf))
    points_sf <- st_transform(points_sf, st_crs(channel_sf))
  
  # ── 1. Dissolve entire network into clean branches ────────────────────────
  # st_line_merge collapses all connected segments into the minimum number
  # of LINESTRING pieces — one per unbranched reach
  network_union  <- st_line_merge(st_union(channel_sf))
  
  # Explode MULTILINESTRING into individual branches
  branches <- st_cast(st_sf(geometry = network_union), "LINESTRING")
  n_branches <- nrow(branches)
  message(sprintf("Dissolved to %d branch(es).", n_branches))
  
  # ── 2. Snap sensor points to network ─────────────────────────────────────
  network_geom  <- st_union(branches)
  nearest_segs  <- st_nearest_points(points_sf, network_geom)
  snapped_pts   <- st_cast(nearest_segs, "POINT")[seq(2, 2*nrow(points_sf), 2)]
  snap_dists    <- as.numeric(st_distance(points_sf,
                                           st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts),
                                           by_element = TRUE))
  
  excluded <- which(snap_dists > snap_tolerance)
  if(length(excluded) > 0)
    warning(sprintf("%d sensor(s) >%gm from network — excluded: rows %s",
                    length(excluded), snap_tolerance,
                    paste(excluded, collapse=",")))
  
  keep       <- which(snap_dists <= snap_tolerance)
  snapped_pts <- snapped_pts[keep]
  points_sf   <- points_sf[keep, ]
  
  message(sprintf("Snapped %d/%d sensor(s) within %gm.",
                  length(keep), nrow(points_sf) + length(excluded), snap_tolerance))
  
  # ── 3. Re-segment each branch ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  # For each branch:
  #   a. Find which snapped sensor points lie on it
  #   b. Compute their fractional positions along the branch
  #   c. Define sensor segment boundaries (±half_seg each side, clamped)
  #   d. Fill gaps with equal-length segments of ≈fill_seg_m
  
  half_seg <- sensor_seg_m / 2
  
  segment_geoms  <- vector("list", n_branches)
  segment_labels <- vector("list", n_branches)  # "sensor" or "fill"
  sensor_idx_map <- vector("list", n_branches)  # which sensor_id per segment
  
  for(b in seq_len(n_branches)){
    branch      <- branches[b, ]
    branch_len  <- as.numeric(st_length(branch))
    
    # Find sensors on this branch
    dist_to_branch <- as.numeric(st_distance(
      st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts), branch
    ))
    on_branch <- which(dist_to_branch <= node_tolerance * 100)
    
    # Compute fractional position of each sensor along the branch
    # using dense sampling for accuracy
    n_samp   <- max(1000L, ceiling(branch_len * 20))
    fracs_s  <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n_samp)
    samples  <- st_cast(st_line_sample(branch, sample = fracs_s), "POINT")
    samp_sfc <- st_sfc(samples, crs = st_crs(branch))
    
    sensor_fracs <- numeric(0)
    sensor_ids   <- integer(0)
    
    for(j in on_branch){
      dists    <- as.numeric(st_distance(snapped_pts[j], samp_sfc))
      frac_pt  <- fracs_s[which.min(dists)]
      sensor_fracs <- c(sensor_fracs, frac_pt)
      sensor_ids   <- c(sensor_ids, j)
    }
    
    # Sort sensors by position along branch (upstream to downstream)
    if(length(sensor_fracs) > 0){
      ord          <- order(sensor_fracs)
      sensor_fracs <- sensor_fracs[ord]
      sensor_ids   <- sensor_ids[ord]
    }
    
    # Build split fraction list:
    # For each sensor: add (frac_center - half/len) and (frac_center + half/len)
    # For remaining gaps: divide equally into chunks of ≈fill_seg_m
    
    all_fracs  <- c(0)
    frac_types <- character(0)  # will label each resulting segment
    seg_sensor <- integer(0)    # sensor index for sensor segments (NA for fill)
    
    # Process intervals: [prev_boundary, next_sensor_lo], sensor seg, [sensor_hi, ...]
    prev_frac <- 0
    
    for(k in seq_along(sensor_fracs)){
      sf_k  <- sensor_fracs[k]
      flo_k <- max(0, sf_k - half_seg / branch_len)
      fhi_k <- min(1, sf_k + half_seg / branch_len)
      
      # Gap before this sensor
      gap_len <- (flo_k - prev_frac) * branch_len
      if(gap_len > node_tolerance){
        # Divide gap into equal fill segments
        n_fill <- max(1L, round(gap_len / fill_seg_m))
        fill_fracs <- seq(prev_frac, flo_k, length.out = n_fill + 1)
        all_fracs  <- c(all_fracs, fill_fracs[-1])  # exclude left (already added)
      }
      
      # Sensor segment boundaries
      all_fracs  <- c(all_fracs, fhi_k)
      prev_frac  <- fhi_k
    }
    
    # Remaining gap after last sensor (or entire branch if no sensors)
    gap_len <- (1 - prev_frac) * branch_len
    if(gap_len > node_tolerance){
      n_fill     <- max(1L, round(gap_len / fill_seg_m))
      fill_fracs <- seq(prev_frac, 1, length.out = n_fill + 1)
      all_fracs  <- c(all_fracs, fill_fracs[-1])
    }
    
    # Ensure 1 is included and fracs are sorted/unique
    all_fracs <- sort(unique(c(all_fracs, 1)))
    # Remove fracs that are too close (would create degenerate segments)
    min_frac_gap <- (node_tolerance * 2) / branch_len
    keep_fracs   <- c(TRUE, diff(all_fracs) > min_frac_gap)
    all_fracs    <- all_fracs[keep_fracs]
    if(tail(all_fracs, 1) < 1) all_fracs <- c(all_fracs, 1)
    
    # Build sub-segment geometries and label them
    segs      <- vector("list", length(all_fracs) - 1)
    seg_type  <- character(length(all_fracs) - 1)
    seg_sid   <- rep(NA_integer_, length(all_fracs) - 1)
    
    for(k in seq_len(length(all_fracs) - 1)){
      f1 <- all_fracs[k]
      f2 <- all_fracs[k + 1]
      
      geom <- tryCatch(
        lwgeom::st_linesubstring(branch, f1, f2),
        error = function(e) NULL
      )
      segs[[k]] <- geom
      
      # Label as sensor if midpoint fraction is inside a sensor window
      mid_frac <- (f1 + f2) / 2
      is_sensor <- FALSE
      for(j in seq_along(sensor_fracs)){
        flo <- max(0, sensor_fracs[j] - half_seg / branch_len)
        fhi <- min(1, sensor_fracs[j] + half_seg / branch_len)
        if(mid_frac >= flo && mid_frac <= fhi){
          is_sensor  <- TRUE
          seg_sid[k] <- sensor_ids[j]
          break
        }
      }
      seg_type[k] <- if(is_sensor) "sensor" else "fill"
    }
    
    valid           <- !sapply(segs, is.null)
    segment_geoms[[b]]  <- segs[valid]
    segment_labels[[b]] <- seg_type[valid]
    sensor_idx_map[[b]] <- seg_sid[valid]
  }
  
  # ── 4. Assemble all segments into one sf object ───────────────────────────
  all_geom_list <- do.call(c, segment_geoms)
  all_types     <- do.call(c, segment_labels)
  all_sensor_ids <- do.call(c, sensor_idx_map)
  
  # Remove null or zero-length results
  valid_geom  <- !sapply(all_geom_list, is.null)
  all_geom_list  <- all_geom_list[valid_geom]
  all_types      <- all_types[valid_geom]
  all_sensor_ids <- all_sensor_ids[valid_geom]
  
  all_segs <- st_sf(
    type       = all_types,
    sensor_idx = all_sensor_ids,
    geometry   = do.call(c, lapply(all_geom_list, st_geometry)),
    crs        = st_crs(channel_sf)
  )
  
  seg_lengths_m <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs))
  all_segs      <- all_segs[seg_lengths_m > node_tolerance, ]
  
  message(sprintf("Created %d segments (%d sensor, %d fill).",
                  nrow(all_segs),
                  sum(all_segs$type == "sensor"),
                  sum(all_segs$type == "fill")))
  
  # ── 5. Build topology (same as before) ────────────────────────────────────
  precision <- max(0, ceiling(-log10(node_tolerance)) + 1)
  n_segs    <- nrow(all_segs)
  
  get_endpoints <- function(geom){
    co <- st_coordinates(geom)
    rbind(round(co[1,        c("X","Y")], precision),
          round(co[nrow(co), c("X","Y")], precision))
  }
  
  ep_matrix   <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(n_segs),
                                        function(i) get_endpoints(all_segs[i,])))
  coord_keys  <- paste(ep_matrix[,"X"], ep_matrix[,"Y"])
  unique_keys <- unique(coord_keys)
  node_ids    <- match(coord_keys, unique_keys)
  start_ids   <- node_ids[seq(1, 2*n_segs, 2)]
  end_ids     <- node_ids[seq(2, 2*n_segs, 2)]
  
  sn_to_row <- setNames(seq_len(n_segs), as.character(start_ids))
  ds        <- unname(sn_to_row[as.character(end_ids)])
  outlets   <- which(is.na(ds))
  
  if(length(outlets) == 0) stop("No outlet found.")
  if(length(outlets) > 1)
    warning(sprintf("%d outlets — network may be disconnected.", length(outlets)))
  
  upstream_of <- vector("list", n_segs)
  for(i in seq_len(n_segs))
    if(!is.na(ds[i])) upstream_of[[ds[i]]] <- c(upstream_of[[ds[i]]], i)
  
  visited <- logical(n_segs); new_order <- integer(0); queue <- outlets
  while(length(queue) > 0){
    cur <- queue[1]; queue <- queue[-1]
    if(!visited[cur]){
      visited[cur] <- TRUE
      new_order    <- c(new_order, cur)
      queue        <- c(queue, upstream_of[[cur]])
    }
  }
  
  unreachable <- setdiff(seq_len(n_segs), new_order)
  if(length(unreachable) > 0)
    warning(sprintf("%d unreachable segment(s).", length(unreachable)))
  
  n_sorted          <- length(new_order)
  topo_id           <- seq_len(n_sorted)
  sorted_start_orig <- start_ids[new_order]
  sorted_end_orig   <- end_ids[new_order]
  sn_orig_to_sorted <- setNames(seq_len(n_sorted), as.character(sorted_start_orig))
  ds_sorted         <- unname(sn_orig_to_sorted[as.character(sorted_end_orig)])
  new_startNode     <- topo_id
  new_endNode       <- ifelse(is.na(ds_sorted), 0L, topo_id[ds_sorted])
  
  violations <- which(new_endNode != 0 & new_endNode >= new_startNode)
  if(length(violations) > 0)
    stop(sprintf("Topology violations at rows: %s", paste(violations, collapse=",")))
  
  sorted_lengths <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs[new_order, ]))
  sorted_types   <- all_segs$type[new_order]
  sorted_sensors <- all_segs$sensor_idx[new_order]
  
  # ── 6. Assemble output ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  channel_df <- data.frame(
    name        = topo_id,
    startNode   = new_startNode,
    endNode     = new_endNode,
    length      = sorted_lengths,
    width       = default_width,
    slope       = default_slope,
    area        = sorted_lengths * default_width,
    type        = sorted_types,
    sensor_idx  = sorted_sensors,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  sorted_geom    <- st_geometry(all_segs[new_order, ])
  channel_sf_out <- st_sf(channel_df, geometry = sorted_geom)
  channel_sv_out <- vect(channel_sf_out)
  crs(channel_sv_out) <- crs(channel_sv)
  
  validate_channel_sv(channel_sv_out, verbose = TRUE)
  
  conv_channel <- convert_channel(
    channel_sv_out,
    property_names = c(name="name", length="length",
                       startNode="startNode", endNode="endNode"),
    default_width = default_width,
    default_slope = default_slope
  )
  
  # ── 7. Sensor-to-HRU mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  id_col <- intersect(c("number","ID","id","sensor_id","name"), names(points_sf))[1]
  
  sensor_map <- data.frame(
    sensor_id  = if(!is.na(id_col)) points_sf[[id_col]] else seq_len(nrow(points_sf)),
    sensor_idx = seq_along(keep),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  hru_map <- channel_df %>%
    filter(type == "sensor", !is.na(sensor_idx)) %>%
    left_join(sensor_map, by = "sensor_idx") %>%
    select(sensor_id, hru_id = name, hru_length = length)
  
  list(
    channel_sv   = channel_sv_out,
    channel_df   = channel_df,
    conv_channel = conv_channel,
    sensor_hru_map = hru_map
  )
}

result <- build_dynatop_channel(
  channel_sv      = vect_final,
  split_points_sv = locs,
  sensor_seg_m    = 10,   # 10m total per sensor (5m each side)
  fill_seg_m      = 100,   # remaining network in ~20m chunks
  snap_tolerance  = 20,
  node_tolerance  = 0.01
)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = st_as_sf(result$channel_sv)) +
    geom_sf(data = locs, color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_as_sf(result$channel_sv), aes(label = name), size = 3) +
  ggtitle("final channel HRUs")+
  theme_void()
# Sensor-to-HRU mapping is now returned directly
sensor_hru_map <- result$sensor_hru_map
print(sensor_hru_map)

# Inspect segment types
table(result$channel_df$type)


writeVector(
  result$conv_channel,
  paste0(processed_folder, "channel.shp"),
  overwrite = TRUE
)

  plot(result$conv_channel)

```


```{r identify ID to hru ID}
# =============================================================================
# Extract sensor-to-HRU mapping from build_dynatop_channel result
# =============================================================================
get_sensor_hru_map <- function(channel_sv, split_points_sv, 
                                buffer_m = 5,
                                node_tolerance = 0.01){
  
  channel_sf <- st_as_sf(channel_sv)
  points_sf  <- st_as_sf(split_points_sv)
  
  if(st_crs(channel_sf) != st_crs(points_sf))
    points_sf <- st_transform(points_sf, st_crs(channel_sf))
  
  # Snap points to channel (same logic as build_dynatop_channel)
  channel_union <- st_union(channel_sf)
  nearest_segs  <- st_nearest_points(points_sf, channel_union)
  snapped_pts   <- st_cast(nearest_segs, "POINT")[seq(2, 2*nrow(points_sf), 2)]
  
  # For each snapped point, find which channel segment it falls within
  # A sensor's segment is the one whose midpoint is closest to the snapped point
  # AND whose length is <= 2*buffer_m + small tolerance (i.e. it's a split segment)
  seg_lengths   <- as.numeric(st_length(channel_sf))
  seg_midpoints <- st_centroid(channel_sf)
  
  mapping <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(points_sf)), function(i){
    
    pt <- snapped_pts[i]
    
    # Distance from snapped point to each segment midpoint
    dists <- as.numeric(st_distance(pt, seg_midpoints))
    
    # Candidate segments: those short enough to be a sensor segment
    # (length <= 2*buffer_m + tolerance) AND closest to the snapped point
    is_sensor_seg <- seg_lengths <= (2 * buffer_m + 1)  # 1m tolerance
    
    if(any(is_sensor_seg)){
      # Among sensor-length segments, pick closest midpoint
      candidate_dists <- ifelse(is_sensor_seg, dists, Inf)
      best_idx <- which.min(candidate_dists)
    } else {
      # Fallback: just pick closest segment overall
      best_idx <- which.min(dists)
    }
    
    # Get sensor ID from the split_points_sv attribute
    # Tries common ID column names — adapt if yours differs
    id_cols <- c("number", "ID", "id", "sensor_id", "name", "sensor_name")
    id_col  <- id_cols[id_cols %in% names(points_sf)][1]
    sensor_id <- if(!is.na(id_col)) points_sf[[id_col]][i] else i
    
    data.frame(
      sensor_id  = sensor_id,
      hru_id     = channel_sf$startNode[best_idx],  # startNode IS the HRU id
      hru_name   = channel_sf$name[best_idx],
      seg_length = round(seg_lengths[best_idx], 2),
      snap_dist  = round(as.numeric(st_distance(
                     points_sf[i,], st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts[i])
                   )), 2),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
  
  rownames(mapping) <- NULL
  return(mapping)
}

# ── Usage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
locs <- vect(stic_locs_snapped)

vect_final <- vect("./5th_attempt/processed/channel.shp")
plot(vect_final)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = st_as_sf(vect_final)) +
    geom_sf(data = locs, color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_as_sf(vect_final), aes(label = name), size = 3) +
  ggtitle("final channel HRUs")+
  theme_void()

sensor_hru_map <- get_sensor_hru_map(
  channel_sv          = vect_final,
  split_points_sv = locs,
  buffer_m        = 5
)


print(sensor_hru_map)
sensor_hru_map$sensor_id <- as.numeric(sensor_hru_map$sensor_id)
#   sensor_id  hru_id  hru_name  seg_length  snap_dist
# 1        A1       4         4        9.87       0.31
# 2        B3      12        12       10.00       0.02
# ...

# Save for use in pa_obs construction
write.csv(sensor_hru_map, "sensor_hru_mapping.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Use directly in pa_obs remapping instead of the distance-based approach
# Replace the left_join(chosen, ...) step with:
# pa_obs <- all_24 %>%
#   filter(wshed == "W3", number %in% sensor_hru_map$sensor_id) %>%
#   left_join(sensor_hru_map, by = c("number" = "sensor_id")) %>%
#   mutate(hru_id = hru_id, ...)
```



# TIMESERIES
```{r}
start <- "2020-01-01"
end <- "2024-11-26"
```

```{r discharge}


# discharge every 15 mins
flow_15min <- read_csv("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/1/18/1b101627265d0b5763fa0bba93d93bf7") 
q_24 <- read_csv("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/VT Research/HBTopModel/w3_discharge_24.csv") %>% 
  mutate(datetime = mdy_hm(datetime)) %>% 
  filter(datetime > max(flow_15min$DATETIME)) %>% 
  mutate(WS = 3,
         Flag = "P",
         Discharge_ls = 28.3168 * Q_cfs) %>% 
  rename(DATETIME = datetime) %>% 
  select(WS, DATETIME, Discharge_ls, Flag)
  

flow_xts <- 
  flow_15min %>%
  rbind(q_24) %>% 
  filter(DATETIME >= start,
         DATETIME <= end) %>% 
  # Create a new column that groups time into 15-minute buckets
  mutate(time_15 = floor_date(DATETIME, "15 mins")) %>%
  # Group by those buckets
  group_by(time_15) %>%
  # Average the 5-minute instantaneous rates to get the 15-minute rate
  summarize(Discharge_ls = mean(Discharge_ls, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  # convert to m3_s, or should it be m3/day?>
  mutate(Discharge_m3_s = Discharge_ls / 1000) %>% 
  as.xts()

flow_xts <- 
  flow_15min %>%
  rbind(q_24) %>% 
  filter(DATETIME >= start,
         DATETIME <= end) %>% 
  # Create a new column that groups time into 15-minute buckets
  mutate(time_hr = floor_date(DATETIME, "1 hour")) %>%
  # Group by those buckets
  group_by(time_hr) %>%
  # Average the 5-minute instantaneous rates to get the 15-minute rate
  summarize(Discharge_ls = mean(Discharge_ls, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  # convert to m3_s, or should it be m3/day?>
  mutate(Discharge_m3_s = Discharge_ls / 1000) %>% 
  as.xts()

```
Units of precipitation and pet are expected to be m accrued over the previous timestep.
```{r precipitation}
precip <- read_csv("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/278/7/132ffe0622ff3f30505c774d24a6b65d")
# precip in mm accrued over timestep, needs to be m accrued over time step

precip_xts <- 
  precip %>% 
  filter(DateTime >= start,
         DateTime <= end) %>% 
  select(DateTime, precip) %>% 
  mutate(precip = precip / 1000) %>% # convert to m
  # Create a new column that groups time into 15-minute buckets
  mutate(time_hr = floor_date(DateTime, "1 hour")) %>%
  # Group by those buckets
  group_by(time_hr) %>%
  # Average the 5-minute instantaneous rates to get the 15-minute rate
  summarize(precip = sum(precip, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  as.xts()
```
```{r pet}
# air temp already 15 min increment
air <- read_csv("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/322/7/a96172a26f381b9ec7005144d12e0820")

air_formatted <- air %>% 
  filter(DateTime >= start,
         DateTime <= end) %>% 
  select(DateTime, airTemp) 

# convert airTemp to pet with hargreaves

lat <- 45   # latitude in decimal degrees (e.g., Blacksburg VA)

# --- Hargreaves PET -----------------------------------------------------

hargreaves_pet <- function(date, temp, lat){

  J <- yday(date)                 # day of year
  phi <- lat * pi/180             # latitude radians
  
  # extraterrestrial radiation (Ra)
  dr  <- 1 + 0.033*cos(2*pi*J/365)
  delta <- 0.409*sin(2*pi*J/365 - 1.39)
  ws <- acos(-tan(phi)*tan(delta))

  Gsc <- 0.0820   # MJ m-2 min-1
  
  Ra <- (24*60/pi) * Gsc * dr *
        (ws*sin(phi)*sin(delta) +
         cos(phi)*cos(delta)*sin(ws))

  PET <- 0.0023 * Ra *
         (temp + 17.8) *
         sqrt(pmax(temp, 0))

  return(PET)   # mm/day
}

# --- Calculate PET ------------------------------------------------------

air_formatted$PET_mm_day <- hargreaves_pet(
  air_formatted$DateTime,
  air_formatted$airTemp,
  lat
)


pet_xts <- 
  air_formatted %>% 
  select(DateTime, PET_mm_day, airTemp) %>% 
  mutate(PET_m_day = PET_mm_day / (1000 * 96)) %>% # convert to m, and 15 min
  rename(pet = PET_m_day) %>% 
  # Create a new column that groups time into 15-minute buckets
  mutate(time_hr = floor_date(DateTime, "1 hour")) %>%
  # Group by those buckets
  group_by(time_hr) %>%
  # Average the 5-minute instantaneous rates to get the 15-minute rate
  summarize(pet = mean(pet, na.rm = TRUE),
            airTemp = mean(airTemp, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  #select() %>% 
  as.xts()

 
```
```{r combine}

obs <- merge(flow_xts[,2], # flow does not go beyond 2024-05-31 23:45:00
             precip_xts,
             pet_xts,
             all=FALSE) ## merge by time stamp
#obs <- obs[ , colSums(is.na(obs)) == 0]               # Remove columns with at least one NA

# Save it
saveRDS(obs, file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "obs.rds"))



#saveRDS(obs, file.path(".", "processed", "obs.rds"))
```

# CREATE DYNATOP OBJECT
```{r create object}
#devtools::install_github("waternumbers/dynatopGIS")
#library(dynatopGIS)

ctch <- dynatopGIS$new(file.path(".", "dynaGISgoodChannel6"))
# add catchment, DEM, channel
ctch$add_catchment(paste0(processed_folder, "w3.tif"))
ctch$add_dem(paste0(processed_folder, "dem.tif"))
ctch$add_channel( paste0(processed_folder, "channel.shp"))

ctch$get_layer()

# plot just to check it out!
ctch$plot_layer("dem", add_channel=TRUE)
```

```{r fill-sinks}
ctch$sink_fill()

terra::plot( ctch$get_layer('filled_dem') - ctch$get_layer('dem'),
             main="Changes to height")
```

```{r compute-stuff}
# bands
ctch$compute_band()
ctch$plot_layer("band")
# twi
ctch$compute_properties()
ctch$plot_layer('atb')
# flow lengths
ctch$compute_flow_lengths(flow_routing="shortest")
ctch$plot_layer("shortest_flow_length")
```

```{r bring in other datasets}

# soil map
soil <- terra::rast("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/380/2/f083871fa0116b17b7398ca48eac7627")
soil_factor <- as.factor(soil)

soil_r  <- resample(project(soil_factor, dem_crop_mask), dem_crop_mask, method="mode")

crop_soil <- crop(soil_r, dem_crop_mask)
mask_soil <- mask(soil_r, dem_crop_mask)
plot(mask_soil)


key <- read_csv("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/soil_depth_planning/HPU_num_key.csv")


levels(mask_soil) <- data.frame(
  "ID" = c(
    5, 4, 2, 8, 3, 1, 6, 7
  ),
  "f083871fa0116b17b7398ca48eac7627" = c(
  "5"," 4"," 2", "8", "3", "1", "6", "7")
)

colors <- c("black",
            "#CCCCCC",
            "#734C00",
            "#FFFF73",
            "#267300",
            "#55FF00",
            "black"
            )
soil_names <- c(
  "Bedrock Histosol",
  "E",
  "Bhs",
  "Typical",
  "Bimodal",
  "Bh"
)
ggplot() +
    geom_spatraster(data = (mask_soil))+
scale_fill_manual(values = colors,
                  labels = soil_names,
                  na.value = NA)+
  theme_void()+
      geom_sf(data = w3_outline, colour = "black", lwd = 1, fill = NA) +
  labs(title = "1 m overburden thickness contours")



# read in krig of depth to bedrock
krig_location <- "/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/soil_depth_planning/krig_output.tif"
k_terra <- rast(krig_location)
plot(k_terra)

# resample to the same pixel size and alignment
depth  <- resample(project(k_terra, dem_crop_mask), dem_crop_mask, method="bilinear") %>% 
  crop(dem_crop_mask) %>% 
  mask(dem_crop_mask)

# check all of their extents
plot(dem_crop_mask)
plot(depth)
plot(mask_soil)


# extend both rasters by 1 pixel
depth_ext <- extend(depth,1)
soil_ext <- extend(mask_soil,1)

# check all of their extents
plot(depth_ext)
plot(soil_ext)
#ctch$plot_layer("dem", add_channel=TRUE)

reclass_matrix <- matrix(c(1, 1,
                           2, 3,
                           3, 2,
                           4, 3,
                           5, 3,
                           7, 3,
                           8, 2), 
                         ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)

# Apply classification
soil_ext <- classify(soil_ext, reclass_matrix)
plot(soil_ext)


ctch$add_layer(depth_ext, layer_name = "depth")
ctch$add_layer(soil_ext, layer_name = "soil")
ctch$classify("depth_5","depth",5)
ctch$plot_layer("depth_5")

```


```{r classifying and cutting}
## simple equally spaced cuts of TWI
ctch$classify("atb_20","atb",20)
ctch$plot_layer("atb_20")

ctch$combine_classes("atb20_soil",c("atb_20","soil"))
ctch$plot_layer("atb20_soil")

tmp <- ctch$get_method("atb20_soil")
tmp$type
#> [1] "combination"
length(tmp$groups$atb5_depth5_soil_band)
```

```{r create the model}
ctch$create_model(file.path("./dynaGISgoodChannel6","model"), # name of new model
                  "atb20_soil", # classification to base model on
                  sf_opt="cnst",
                  sz_opt="exp"
                  )

## load the model
mdl <- readRDS( file.path(".","dynaGISgoodChannel6","model.rds")) ## read the model in
obs <- readRDS(file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "obs.rds"))
vect_final <- vect("./5th_attempt/processed/channel.shp")

names(obs) <- c("Discharge_m3_s", "precip","pet","temp_series" )
```

```{r fix hru topology}
fix_hru_topology <- function(mdl, verbose = TRUE){
  hru <- mdl$hru
  
  # ── Rename helper — updates id AND all references ────────────────────────
  rename_hru <- function(hru, old_id, new_id){
    for(ii in seq_along(hru)){
      if(hru[[ii]]$id == old_id) hru[[ii]]$id <- new_id
      hru[[ii]]$sf_flow_direction$id[
        hru[[ii]]$sf_flow_direction$id == old_id] <- new_id
      hru[[ii]]$sz_flow_direction$id[
        hru[[ii]]$sz_flow_direction$id == old_id] <- new_id
    }
    return(hru)
  }
  
  # ── Find violations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  find_violations <- function(hru){
    out <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(hru), function(ii){
      h      <- hru[[ii]]
      bad_sf <- h$sf_flow_direction$id[h$sf_flow_direction$id >= h$id]
      bad_sz <- h$sz_flow_direction$id[h$sz_flow_direction$id >= h$id]
      if(length(bad_sf) > 0 || length(bad_sz) > 0)
        data.frame(hru_id  = h$id,
                   max_bad = max(c(bad_sf, bad_sz)),
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }))
    out
  }
  
  # ── Iterative fix ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Strategy: find HRU X that flows to Y where Y >= X.
  # X must have id > Y (and > everything else X flows to).
  # New id for X = max(all downstream ids of X) + 1.
  # Every HRU currently occupying ids between old_X and new_X shifts down by 1
  # to keep the sequence contiguous.
  
  tmp_id  <- 9999L  # safe scratch id above any real id
  max_iter <- 50L
  
  for(iter in seq_len(max_iter)){
    violations <- find_violations(hru)
    if(is.null(violations) || nrow(violations) == 0){
      if(verbose) cat(sprintf("All violations resolved after %d iteration(s).\n",
                              iter - 1))
      break
    }
    if(verbose){
      cat(sprintf("Iteration %d — violations:\n", iter))
      print(violations)
    }
    
    # Fix the first violation
    v      <- violations[1, ]
    hru_id <- v$hru_id
    
    # Collect ALL downstream ids for this HRU (sf and sz)
    ii      <- which(sapply(hru, function(h) h$id == hru_id))
    all_dst <- c(hru[[ii]]$sf_flow_direction$id,
                 hru[[ii]]$sz_flow_direction$id)
    all_dst <- all_dst[!is.na(all_dst) & length(all_dst) > 0]
    
    # New id must be strictly above all downstream ids
    new_id  <- max(all_dst) + 1L
    old_id  <- hru_id
    
    if(verbose)
      cat(sprintf("  Moving HRU %d -> %d (flows to: %s)\n",
                  old_id, new_id, paste(all_dst, collapse=",")))
    
    # Step 1: park the offending HRU at tmp_id
    hru <- rename_hru(hru, old_id, tmp_id)
    
    # Step 2: shift every HRU with id in (old_id, new_id] down by 1
    # Do this from lowest to highest to avoid collisions
    ids_to_shift <- sort(sapply(hru, function(h) h$id))
    ids_to_shift <- ids_to_shift[ids_to_shift > old_id &
                                   ids_to_shift <= new_id &
                                   ids_to_shift != tmp_id]
    
    for(shift_id in ids_to_shift){
      hru <- rename_hru(hru, shift_id, shift_id - 1L)
    }
    
    # Step 3: move parked HRU from tmp_id to new_id
    hru <- rename_hru(hru, tmp_id, new_id)
  }
  
  if(iter == max_iter){
    remaining <- find_violations(hru)
    if(!is.null(remaining) && nrow(remaining) > 0){
      warning("Could not resolve all violations after ", max_iter,
              " iterations. Remaining:\n")
      print(remaining)
      cat("This may indicate a cycle in the channel network.\n")
    }
  }
  
  # ── Verify contiguous sequence ────────────────────────────────────────────
  final_ids <- sort(sapply(hru, function(h) h$id))
  expected  <- seq(min(final_ids), max(final_ids))
  gaps      <- setdiff(expected, final_ids)
  if(length(gaps) > 0){
    warning("Gaps in id sequence after fix: ", paste(gaps, collapse=", "))
  } else {
    if(verbose) cat("ID sequence contiguous: OK\n")
  }
  
  mdl$hru <- hru
  return(mdl)
}

mdl <- fix_hru_topology(mdl = mdl, verbose = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# actually need to figure out which HRU in final model corresponds with sensor location

# write function to filter HRU object for HRU's with a specific class name.
sensor_hru_map
mdl$hru[[1]]$class$name

hid <- sapply(mdl$hru,function(h){h$id})
## create a vector of the channel names with as storaged in the class variabel
cname <- sapply(mdl$hru,function(h){h$class$name})
idx <- match(sensor_hru_map$hru_name,cname) ## index of HRU's corresponding to gauges
## create as a data.frame
sensor_hru_key <- data.frame(sensor_id = sensor_hru_map$sensor_id,
           hru_id = as.integer(hid[idx]),
           name = sensor_hru_map$hru_name
                           )
```

```{r identify soil groups}
# =============================================================================
# EXTRACT SOIL GROUPS FOR HILLSLOPE HRUs
# =============================================================================
# Assuming 'soil' was used to generate your HRU classes and is stored in $class
# You may need to change `h$class$soil` to wherever your class name is stored 
# (e.g., h$properties["soil"] or similar).

hru_soil_map <- lapply(seq_along(mdl$hru), function(ii) {
  h <- mdl$hru[[ii]]
  is_channel <- !is.na(h$class$endNode)
  
  if (!is_channel) {
    # CHANGE THIS to the exact location of your soil identifier
    soil_id <- as.character(h$class$soil) 
    data.frame(hru_index = ii, hru_id = h$id, soil_group = soil_id)
  } else {
    NULL
  }
})

hru_soil_map <- do.call(rbind, hru_soil_map)

# Map the raw soil names/IDs to a simple index (1, 2, 3) for parameter matching
unique_soils <- unique(hru_soil_map$soil_group)
hru_soil_map$soil_idx <- match(hru_soil_map$soil_group, unique_soils)

cat("Found", length(unique_soils), "soil groups across", nrow(hru_soil_map), "hillslope HRUs.\n")
```


# RUN MODEL
```{r add snow parameters to model object}
# 2. Patch the HRUs with snow parameters, temp linkages, and expanded states
for (i in seq_along(mdl$hru)) {
  
  # A. Add the snow parameters
  mdl$hru[[i]]$snow <- list(
    type = "hbv",
    parameters = c(T_T = 0.0, C_f = 2.5, C_wh = 0.1)
  )
  
  # B. Add the temp linkage 
  # Note: The original precip linkage uses 'name' and 'fraction'
  # We will map it to a dummy "temp_series"
  mdl$hru[[i]]$temp <- list(
    name = "temp_series",
    fraction = 1.0
  )
  
  # C. Expand the states to include s_snow and s_liquid
  mdl$hru[[i]]$states <- c(
    mdl$hru[[i]]$states, 
    s_snow = 0.0, 
    s_liquid = 0.0
  )
}

# fpar <- function(h){ ## h is the hru
#     ## test if a channel
#     if( !is.na(h$class$startNode) ){
#         ## change type
#         h$sf$type <- "cnst"
#         h$sf$parameters["csf"] <- 1
#     }
#     h
# }
# mdl$hru <- lapply(mdl$hru, fpar)

# function to set snow states to zero

fix_snow <- function(mdl){
  for(ii in seq_along(mdl)){
      mdl[[ii]]$states$s_snow <- 0
      mdl[[ii]]$states$s_liquid <- 0
  }
  return(mdl)
}

mdl_after2 <- fix_snow(mdl_after)
```

```{r plot HRUS}

hru_map <- rast(mdl$map) 

# Rename the raster layer so we can call it easily in ggplot
names(hru_map) <- "hru_id"

# 3. Convert the raster to polygons to calculate the center of each HRU
hru_poly <- as.polygons(hru_map)

# Calculate the centroids (center points) of each polygon for the text labels
hru_centroids <- centroids(hru_poly)

# Extract coordinates into a standard dataframe for ggplot
centroid_coords <- crds(hru_centroids, df = TRUE)
centroid_coords$hru_id <- hru_centroids$hru_id
              # 4. Plot using tidyterra and ggplot2
channels <- centroid_coords %>% 
  filter(hru_id %in% unique(sensor_hru_map$hru_id))

get_channel_hrus <- function(mdl) {
  #if (is.character(mdl)) mdl <- readRDS(mdl)
  mdl$channel[, c("id", "area")]
}

test <- get_channel_hrus(mdl)
mdl$hru[, c("id", "area")]
# fpar <- function(h){ ## h is the hru
#     ## test if a channel
#     if( !is.na(h$class$startNode) ){
#         ## change type
#         h$sf$type <- "cnst"
#         h$sf$parameters["csf"] <- 1
#     }
#     h
# }
# mdl$hru <- lapply(mdl$hru, fpar)

ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatraster(data = as.factor(hru_map)) +
  # Overlay the polygon boundaries
  #geom_spatvector(data = hru_poly, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.2) +
  # Add the ID labels at the center of each HRU
  geom_text(data = channels, aes(x = x, y = y, label = hru_id),
            color = "black", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  # Use a nice color palette and remove the bulky legend
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", na.value = "transparent", guide = "none") +
  # lims(x = c(281500, 281700),
  #      y = c(4870400, 4870500))+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Hubbard Brook HRU Map")+
  theme_void()
#ggsave("HRU.png", scale = 3)
```

```{r test run model}
# Load previously created data
output_defn <- data.frame(name = c("q_sf", "v_sf_rz"),
                      id = 0,
                      flux = c("s_sf", "v_sf_rz"),
                      scale = c(1, -1/900))
#mdl <- readRDS(file.path(".","dynaGIS","atb_20.rds"))
#obs <- readRDS(file.path(".","processed","obs.rds"))
# initialize dynatop object

## determining the initial recharge rate ad setting it in the model
# I think the same number is the watershed area...
#r0 <- as.numeric(obs[1, "Streamflow"])#/ 86400
#r0 <- as.numeric(obs$Streamflow[1])
r0 <- as.numeric(obs$Discharge_m3_s[1])/ sum( sapply( mdl$hru, function(x){x$properties["area"]} ) )
mdl$hru <- lapply(mdl$hru,function(h){h$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"] <- r0;h})

#start <- "2020-01-01"
#end <- "2020-01-31"

cal_start    <- as.POSIXct(start, tz = "UTC")
cal_end      <- as.POSIXct(end, tz = "UTC")

obs_sim      <- obs[index(obs) >= cal_start & index(obs) <= cal_end, ]
obs_q        <- obs_sim[, "Discharge_m3_s"]
## the map input is only needed for visualisation
Sys.time()
ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(mdl$hru, map = mdl$map
  )$add_data( obs_sim
  )$initialise(
  )$sim(mdl$output_flux, sub_step = 3600
  )


mdl_after <- ctch_mdl$get_model()

mdl_after[[1]]$states
# Gauge Flows
sim_gauge <- ctch_mdl$get_output() ## extract the flow as an xts object
sim_gauge[1:50,]
plot(sim_gauge[,1],main="Simulated and observed flows",
     legend.loc='topright',
     xlim = c( as.POSIXct("2020-01-03", tz = "UTC")))
plot(merge(sim_gauge[,1], obs_q$Discharge_m3_s),main="Simulated and observed flows",
     legend.loc='topright')


```

```{r}
# ── 1. Check if rapply deep copy is actually in run_model ─────────────────────
# Print first few lines of run_model body to confirm
head(deparse(body(run_model)), 20)
# Must show: hru <- rapply(mdl_template$hru, function(x) x, how = "replace")
# If it shows: hru <- mdl_template$hru   ← this is the bug

# ── 2. Compare initialization values ─────────────────────────────────────────
# What does run_model set vs what the default model has?
cat("Default s_rz_0:   ", mdl$hru[[1]]$initialisation["s_rz_0"], "\n")
cat("Default r_uz_sz_0:", mdl$hru[[1]]$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"], "\n")

# What does run_model compute?
total_area <- sum(sapply(mdl$hru, function(h) h$properties["area"]))
q_init     <- as.numeric(na.omit(obs_sim$Discharge_m3_s))[1]
cat("run_model s_rz_0:   ", 0.1, "\n")
cat("run_model r_uz_sz_0:", q_init / total_area, "\n")
cat("q_init:", q_init, "\n")
cat("total_area:", total_area, "\n")

# ── 3. Check what parameters run_model actually applies ───────────────────────
# Temporarily add print statements to run_model:
default_params <- c(t_0=0.135, m=0.040, t_d=28800, s_rzmax=0.1,
                    c_sf=0.3, n_ch=0.05, v_ch=1.0,
                    T_T=0.0, C_f=0.002, C_wh=1.0)

# Check one hillslope and one channel HRU after parameter setting
hru_test <- rapply(mdl$hru, function(x) x, how = "replace")
is_channel_1 <- isTRUE(!is.na(hru_test[[1]]$class$endNode))
cat("HRU 1 is channel:", is_channel_1, "\n")

# Apply params manually as run_model would
for(ii in seq_along(hru_test)){
  is_channel <- isTRUE(!is.na(hru_test[[ii]]$class$endNode))
  if(!is_channel){
    hru_test[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- default_params["t_0"]
    hru_test[[ii]]$sz$parameters["m"]       <- default_params["m"]
    hru_test[[ii]]$uz$parameters["t_d"]     <- default_params["t_d"]
    hru_test[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- default_params["s_rzmax"]
    if(hru_test[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst")
      hru_test[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- default_params["c_sf"]
  } else {
    hru_test[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- 0.001
    hru_test[[ii]]$sz$parameters["m"]       <- 0.001
    hru_test[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- 0.001
    if(hru_test[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst"){
      hru_test[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- default_params["v_ch"]
      hru_test[[ii]]$sf$parameters["t_raf"] <- 999.9
    }
  }
}

# Print a hillslope and channel HRU to verify
hs_idx <- which(!sapply(hru_test, function(h) isTRUE(!is.na(h$class$endNode))))[1]
ch_idx <- which( sapply(hru_test, function(h) isTRUE(!is.na(h$class$endNode))))[1]

cat("\nHillslope HRU", hru_test[[hs_idx]]$id, "after param setting:\n")
cat("  sz:", hru_test[[hs_idx]]$sz$parameters, "\n")
cat("  uz:", hru_test[[hs_idx]]$uz$parameters, "\n")
cat("  rz:", hru_test[[hs_idx]]$rz$parameters, "\n")
cat("  sf:", hru_test[[hs_idx]]$sf$parameters, "\n")

cat("\nChannel HRU", hru_test[[ch_idx]]$id, "after param setting:\n")
cat("  sz:", hru_test[[ch_idx]]$sz$parameters, "\n")
cat("  rz:", hru_test[[ch_idx]]$rz$parameters, "\n")
cat("  sf:", hru_test[[ch_idx]]$sf$parameters, "\n")

# ── 4. Compare obs passed to run_model vs default run ─────────────────────────
cat("\nobs_sim columns:", names(obs_sim), "\n")
cat("obs_sim nrow:", nrow(obs_sim), "\n")
```
